report:
VisionGuard inspected 113 product images this shift and detected 40 potential defects, giving a defect rate of 35.4%.

The most common defect type was "crack" and the most common defect category was "surface crack". The highest severity observed was "critical". Average Qwen-VL inference latency was 1318ms per image on AMD MI300X.

The likely recurring causes include excess pressure during molding, thermal stress during cooling, impact damage during handling. Recommended action: pause the affected line, inspect the nearby batch, and review process settings before continuing full-speed production. Prevention summary: reduce mechanical impact during transfer, calibrate temperature control, add automated inspection after cooling stage.
operations_alert:
Operations Alert:
Crack-related defects increased in the latest inspection batch.

Risk:
Critical or warning-level defects are concentrated on Line LINE-A1.

Most common likely cause:
excess pressure during molding, thermal stress during cooling, impact damage during handling.

Recommended immediate action:
inspect nearby batch items, remove this item from the production line, check molding pressure and cooling temperature.

Prevention:
reduce mechanical impact during transfer, calibrate temperature control, add automated inspection after cooling stage.
